rules.name: unwrap-usage
rules.message: unwrap() usage — can panic at runtime if the value is None or Err.
rules.fix_hint: Use pattern matching, unwrap_or, unwrap_or_else, or the ? operator instead.

rules.name: expect-usage
rules.message: expect() usage — better than unwrap but still panics on None/Err.
rules.fix_hint: Prefer the ? operator or match/if-let for recoverable errors; reserve expect for truly unrecoverable cases.

rules.name: unsafe-block
rules.message: Unsafe block — bypasses Rust's safety guarantees and requires manual correctness proof.
rules.fix_hint: Verify the unsafe block is necessary; document the safety invariant with a // SAFETY: comment.

rules.name: hardcoded-credentials
rules.fix_hint: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager; load via std::env::var or config crate.

rules.name: console-output
rules.message: Console output — use a structured logger (tracing, log crate) instead.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with tracing::info!/debug!/warn! or log::info!/debug!/warn! macros.

rules.name: excessive-clone
rules.message: clone() call — may indicate unnecessary data copying.
rules.fix_hint: Consider borrowing (&T) or using Cow<T> to avoid unnecessary allocations.

rules.name: deep-nesting
rules.message: Deep nesting detected (4+ indent levels) — reduces readability.
rules.fix_hint: Extract inner logic into named functions or use early returns / guard clauses.

rules.name: magic-numbers
rules.message: Magic number in comparison — extract to a named constant for clarity.
rules.fix_hint: Define a const with a descriptive name.

file_size._note: Lines of code. Test and benchmark files get more room.

function_size._note: Detected via brace-counting heuristic, not grep.
